loanwords.sanskrit_gloss: city/settlement suffix in Deccan/South Indian toponyms
loanwords.significance: Sanskrit toponym suffix '-ūr' ← PDr *ūr = M233 'ūr' (HIGH). Hundreds of South Indian place-names: Thanjavur, Nagpur, Bengaluru.
Sanskrit 'annam' ← PDr *an(ṉ) = M176 'an/aṇ' (HIGH). No IE internal etymology; Dravidian source confirmed.

loanwords.sanskrit_gloss: corner, angle; in compounds: chief, head
loanwords.significance: Sanskrit 'koṇa' (corner/chief) ← PDr *kōṉ (king) = M073 (HIGH). Title semantics preserved.
Sanskrit dative '-āya' ← influenced by PDr oblique *āy = M342 'ay/ā' (HIGH). Case suffix parallelism.
Sanskrit 'mīna' (fish/Pisces) ← PDr *mīn = M047 'min/mīn' (MEDIUM). Direct loanword, no IE etymology.
Sanskrit 'kuṭi' ← PDr *kuṭi (family/clan shelter) = P324 candidate. Triangulates Phase-234 reading.
Sanskrit 'vāṇija' (merchant) ← PDr *vāṇijaṉ. Trade vocabulary transfer.
Sanskrit 'nīla' (indigo) ← PDr *nīl (dark color). Indigo was Harappan trade good.
Sanskrit 'ēruṣa' (bull) ← PDr *erutu/*erumai = M062 'erutu' (HIGH). Animal determinative confirmed externally.
Sanskrit 'yāna' (vehicle/elephant) ← PDr *yānai = M045 'yānai' (HIGH). Elephant term confirmed.
Sanskrit 'naṭa' (dancer/performer) ← PDr *naṭu. Cultural term transfer.
Sanskrit 'kaṭa' (mat/wicker) ← PDr *kaṭ. Craft vocabulary transfer.
Sanskrit 'citta' (mind) has Dravidian cognate. Abstract concept borrowing.
Sanskrit 'pala' (weight unit) ← PDr *pal. Harappan weight system vocabulary.

loanwords.sanskrit_gloss: gem, jewel, bead; also: weight measure
loanwords.significance: Sanskrit 'maṇi' (gem/weight) ← PDr *maṇi. Akkadian 'mana' (mina) also borrows. IB-G01 weight system candidate.
Sanskrit south/swift term borrowed from PDr directional vocabulary.
Sanskrit 'kaṇa' (eye/particle) ← PDr *kaṇ (eye). Body vocabulary borrowing.

loanwords.sanskrit_gloss: debt, loan; genitive marker in compounds
loanwords.significance: Sanskrit compound genitive 'iṇa-' ← PDr genitive *iN = M267 'iN/in' (MEDIUM). Grammatical particle preserved.
Sanskrit 'kāṭu' (forest) ← PDr *kāṭu. Landscape vocabulary transfer.

Sanskrit 'aṇṇā' (elder brother) ← PDr kinship term *aṇṇan. Same root as M176 'an/aṇ' personal suffix (HIGH).
Sanskrit rain/year term partially from PDr seasonal vocabulary.

loanwords.sanskrit_gloss: ear; also: wheel spoke, rudder (navigator role)
loanwords.significance: Sanskrit 'karṇa' (ear/rudder) has PDr phonological influence in retroflex.

loanwords.sanskrit_gloss: river name; also: pool, water body
loanwords.significance: Rgvedic river name Sarasvati = PDr *sar (water-body) + IE suffix. Harappan substratum toponym (Witzel 1999).

loanwords.sanskrit_gloss: name of a people/territory (Kuru kingdom); also: do, perform
loanwords.significance: Sanskrit 'Kuru' (kingdom name) may encode PDr *kur. M122 LOW='kur' — external corroboration.
Sanskrit regional term 'eruma' (buffalo) ← PDr *erumai = M008 'erumai' (HIGH). Phase-216 upgrade confirmed externally.

loanwords.sanskrit_gloss: strip of cloth, charter, royal document/seal
loanwords.significance: Sanskrit 'paṭṭa' (royal seal/document) ← PDr *paṭṭam. Administrative vocabulary — directly relevant to seal-making culture.
Sanskrit 'iñcī' (ginger) ← PDr *iñci = M168 'inci' (HIGH, Phase-216). Agricultural crop word confirmed.

loanwords.sanskrit_gloss: play, drama; belonging to a territory (nāṭa-)
loanwords.significance: Sanskrit 'nāṭaka' ← PDr *nāṭu (land/territory). Administrative/political term borrowing.
